metadata.publisher: Government Printer; Tanzania Revenue Authority laws library print
metadata.source_authority: United Republic of Tanzania (Attorney General's Chambers Revised Edition)
metadata.source_note: Tanzania Revenue Authority laws-library print of the Income Tax Act (Cap. 332, Revised Edition 2023; 146-page text-embedded consolidation). Grounds the First Schedule paragraph 1(1) resident individual annual table (nil where total income does not exceed 3,240,000 shillings; 8 percent of the excess to 6,240,000; 240,000 plus 20 percent to 9,120,000; 816,000 plus 25 percent to 12,000,000; 1,536,000 plus 30 percent above), the paragraph 2 presumptive regime (conditions including the independent-professions carve-out; the 100,000,000 threshold; the FA2022 table with the incomplete-records flat amounts 100,000/250,000, the with-records 3 percent arms and the 3.5 percent of turnover band for 11,000,001 to 100,000,000), and the charging and employment-income provisions (sections 4 and 7). The rate history: the thresholds date to the Finance Act 2020 and the 8 percent second band to the Finance Act 2021 (both captured alongside for dating).
metadata.title: Income Tax Act (Cap. 332, R.E. 2023) - First Schedule paragraph 1(1) individual rates and paragraph 2 presumptive regime; sections 4 and 7

metadata.publisher: Government Printer (Gazette supplement); Tanzania Communications Regulatory Authority document library
metadata.source_authority: United Republic of Tanzania (Parliament)
metadata.source_note: Tanzania Communications Regulatory Authority library print of the Finance Act, 2021 (No. 3 of 2021; text-embedded print whose file begins with a stray leading space byte before the PDF magic - harmless to extraction). Grounds section 25 (the substituted First Schedule paragraph 1(1) individual table with the 8 percent second band and cumulative amounts 240,000 / 816,000 / 1,536,000, in force from 1 July 2021 - the schedule the TAZMOD TZ_2023 system applies), sections 42-43 (the Road and Fuel Tolls Act (Cap. 220) Second Schedule toll raised to 413 shillings per litre of petrol and diesel), and section 11 (the restructured spirits heading 22.08: locally produced 3,978 and imported 4,386 shillings per litre - the rows the TAZMOD spirits figure of 3,655.05 predates, a recorded parity finding).
metadata.title: Finance Act, 2021 (No. 3 of 2021) - section 25 individual rate table (8 percent second band); sections 42-43 fuel toll 413 per litre; section 11 spirits heading

metadata.publisher: Government Printer (Gazette supplement); Ministry of Finance document library
metadata.source_authority: United Republic of Tanzania (Parliament)
metadata.source_note: Ministry of Finance library print of the Finance Act, 2022 (No. 5 of 2022; 48-page text-embedded print). Grounds section 72: the substituted First Schedule paragraph 2 presumptive table (nil to 4,000,000; flat 100,000 without complete records or 3 percent of the excess over 4,000,000 with records; flat 250,000 or 90,000 plus 3 percent of the excess over 7,000,000; 3.5 percent of turnover for 11,000,001 to 100,000,000) and the paragraph 2(1) carve-out of independent professions and technical/management/construction/training service providers - the presumptive state the TAZMOD TZ_2023 system models (marginally, a recorded finding on the whole-turnover 3.5 percent band). Part IX adds excise items without touching the modelled rows.
metadata.title: Finance Act, 2022 (No. 5 of 2022) - section 72 presumptive table substitution and independent-professions carve-out

metadata.publisher: Government Printer (Act Supplement No. 2, gazetted 30 June 2023); Tanzania Revenue Authority laws library
metadata.source_authority: United Republic of Tanzania (Parliament)
metadata.source_note: Tanzania Revenue Authority laws-library print of the Finance Act, 2023 (No. 7 of 2023; 49-page text-embedded print). Grounds section 10 (the Fourth Schedule uplifts commencing 1 July 2023: beer of heading 22.03 to 918 shillings per litre; cigarettes with filter to 35,310 shillings per mille; other manufactured tobacco to 33,878.88 shillings per kilogramme) and sections 50-51 (the Road and Fuel Tolls Act (Cap. 220) toll raised from 413 to 513 shillings per litre). These are the rates the TAZMOD TZ_2023 system applies under its 1-July point-in-time convention (fuel: petrol 379 excise + 513 toll = 892; gas oil 255 + 513 = 768).
metadata.title: Finance Act, 2023 (No. 7 of 2023) - section 10 excise uplifts (beer 918; cigarettes 35,310 per mille; tobacco 33,878.88 per kg); sections 50-51 fuel toll 513 per litre

metadata.publisher: Government Printer; National Social Security Fund publication library
metadata.source_authority: United Republic of Tanzania (Attorney General's Chambers Revised Edition)
metadata.source_note: National Social Security Fund library print of the NSSF Act (Cap. 50, Revised Edition 2023; 50-page text-embedded consolidation; the administering institution's own copy of the Revised Edition, host recorded). Grounds section 12(1) (the employer pays a contribution for every contribution period) and the First Schedule contribution table: the statutory contribution for each employee is twenty cents for every complete shilling of wages (20 percent) of which the employer's share deductible from wages is ten cents (the 10 percent employee share), leaving the employer to fund the other 10 percent. TAZMOD TZ_2023 applies 5 percent employee plus 5 percent employer - half the statutory rate, a recorded parity finding.
metadata.title: National Social Security Fund Act (Cap. 50, R.E. 2023) - section 12 and the First Schedule contribution table (twenty cents per complete shilling of wages; ten-cent deductible employee share)

metadata.publisher: Government Printer; Ministry of Labour (kazi.go.tz) document library
metadata.source_authority: United Republic of Tanzania (Parliament)
metadata.source_note: Ministry of Labour library print of the Public Service Social Security Fund Act, 2018 (No. 2 of 2018; 69-page text-embedded print; the file name carries a stray _sw suffix but the text is the English Act). Grounds section 18: a contribution payable to the Fund by the employer at the rate of twenty percentum of the employee's monthly salary, constituted by five percentum deducted from the member's salary and fifteen percentum contributed by the employer, with the employee share capped at fifty percent of the total. TAZMOD TZ_2023 applies exactly this 5 + 15 split to public servants.
metadata.title: Public Service Social Security Fund Act, 2018 (No. 2 of 2018) - section 18 contributions (20 percent of monthly salary; 5 percent member + 15 percent employer)

metadata.publisher: Government Printer; Ministry of Labour (kazi.go.tz) document library

metadata.publisher: Government Printer; Ministry of Health document library
metadata.source_authority: United Republic of Tanzania (Attorney General's Chambers Revised Edition)
metadata.source_note: Ministry of Health library print of the National Health Insurance Fund Act (Cap. 395, Revised Edition 2023; 30-page text-embedded consolidation). Grounds section 9: an employer makes a monthly contribution to the Fund of an equivalency of three per centum of the employee's salary, and the Treasury deposits the employee's monthly contribution together with another three per centum from the employer; section 10 lets the Board review the rate subject to regulation. TAZMOD TZ_2023 applies 3 + 3 percent to formal employees.
metadata.title: National Health Insurance Fund Act (Cap. 395, R.E. 2023) - section 9 contributions (employer three per centum plus a matching employee three per centum)

metadata.publisher: Government Printer; Ministry of Health document library
metadata.source_authority: United Republic of Tanzania (Attorney General's Chambers Revised Edition)

metadata.publisher: Government Printer; Tanzania Revenue Authority laws library
metadata.source_authority: United Republic of Tanzania (Attorney General's Chambers Revised Edition)
metadata.source_note: Tanzania Revenue Authority laws-library print of the Value Added Tax Act (Cap. 148, Revised Edition 2023; 90-page text-embedded consolidation). Grounds section 5(1): the amount of value added tax payable is calculated by multiplying the value of the supply or import by the value added tax rate, which shall be eighteen percent (mainland Tanzania; unchanged since the Value Added Tax Act, 2014). TAZMOD TZ_2023 applies the 18 percent rate on its VAT-base expenditure list and on the excise-inclusive value of excisable purchases.
metadata.title: Value Added Tax Act (Cap. 148, R.E. 2023) - section 5(1) rate (eighteen percent)

metadata.publisher: Government Printer; Ministry of Finance document library
metadata.source_authority: United Republic of Tanzania (Attorney General's Chambers Revised Edition)
metadata.source_note: Ministry of Finance library print of the Excise (Management and Tariff) Act (Cap. 147, Revised Edition 2019; 112-page text-embedded consolidation). Grounds the Fourth Schedule base rows the Finance Acts amend: wine of heading 22.04 "other" at 2,466.45 shillings per litre (unamended through the Finance Act 2023 - the TAZMOD wine figure of 2,959.74 matches no print, a recorded parity finding); petrol at 379 and gas oil at 255 shillings per litre (the excise components under the Road and Fuel Tolls Act toll); cigars at 30 percent; and the pre-FA2021 spirits and pre-FA2023 beer, cigarette and tobacco figures for the amendment chain.
metadata.title: Excise (Management and Tariff) Act (Cap. 147, R.E. 2019) - Fourth Schedule base rows (wine 2,466.45 per litre; petrol 379 and gas oil 255 per litre; cigars 30 percent; the pre-uplift beer, cigarette, tobacco and spirits figures)

metadata.publisher: Government Printer; Tanzania Revenue Authority laws library
metadata.source_authority: United Republic of Tanzania (Attorney General's Chambers Revised Edition)
metadata.source_note: Tanzania Revenue Authority laws-library print of the Income Tax Act (Cap. 332, Revised Edition 2019; 143-page text-embedded consolidation) - the superseded edition carrying the pre-FA2020 individual table (nil band 2,040,000), the original presumptive table and the paragraph 2(2) threshold of 100,000,000 shillings, and the section 4(5) presumptive routing. Captured for the amendment chain behind the R.E. 2023 consolidation; the operative TZ_2023 rates come from the R.E. 2023 print and the Finance Acts 2021-2023 captured alongside.
metadata.title: Income Tax Act (Cap. 332, R.E. 2019) - superseded consolidation (pre-FA2020 individual table; original presumptive table and the 100,000,000 threshold) for the amendment chain
